What is the main landmass of the Australian continent, excluding Tasmania, New Guinea, and other offshore islands?
Mainland Australia
How long ago did Australian Aborigines occupy mainland Australia?
up to 50,000 to 60,000 years ago
When did Australia completely separate from Antarctica?
about 53 million years ago
Who was the first documented European to encounter the Australian mainland?
Willem Janszoon
What tectonic plate is Australia situated on?
Indo-Australian tectonic plate
What has caused the physical changes in the Australian mainland?
The shifting of tectonic plates.
How long does the Southern Highlands experience snowfall during winter?
more than half the year
What percentage of the Australian mainland do the Central Lowlands occupy?
approximately 25%
What are the three primary drainage basins of the Central Lowlands?
Carpentaria Lowlands, Lake Eyre, and Murray-Darling Basin.
What is the elevation of Lake Eyre relative to sea level?
50 meters below sea level
During which geological period was the Western Plateau formed?
Cretaceous period
What types of rock make up plateaus?
Igneous and metamorphic rock
What is the average elevation of the Plateaus above sea level?
500 meters
What are some significant features of the Plateaus?
Uluru, Kata Tjuta, the Finke River, the Victorian Desert, the Great Sandy Desert, and the Gibson Desert.
What color does the Western Plateau appear?
red
When does the Northern Plateau experience heavy rainfall?
From November to April.
In which region is Northern Plateau located compared to Western Plateau?
a more tropical region
What processes have contributed to the accumulation of Australia's mineral, oil, and gas resources?
uplift, erosion, and thick terrigenous deposition over millions of years
What are some of the minerals and ores that Australia produces?
iron ore, coal, bauxite, manganese, nickel, copper, gold, silver, cobalt, lead, uranium, opal, and diamond
What sources primarily contributed to the renewable energy generation in mainland Australia in 2021?
wind and solar
Has the Liberal National opposition ever controlled the entirety of Mainland Australia at the federal and mainland state levels simultaneously as of 2024?
No.
When was Canberra designated as the national capital?
March 12, 1913
